message:
refactor(core): redesign LFU cache internals with O(1) linked frequency buckets

Replace the Vec-based frequency bucket implementation with a doubly-linked
list threaded through the cache entries themselves, achieving true O(1)
amortized time for all operations (get, insert, evict) regardless of
the number of entries sharing a frequency.

What changed and why:
- CacheEntry<V> → CacheEntry<K, V> with prev/next pointers for
  intra-bucket linked list traversal, eliminating O(n) Vec::remove
  during frequency promotion
- New FrequencyBucket<K> struct maintains head/tail/len plus
  prev_frequency/next_frequency links for O(1) min-frequency tracking
  without scanning the HashMap
- get() refactored to avoid double HashMap lookup: extracts old_frequency
  first, then promotes in a single pass through the linked structure
- Eviction now unlinks the tail of the min-frequency bucket in O(1)
  instead of Vec::remove(0)

Proptest updates:
- Fix docstring: "contains_key agrees with peek" (was incorrectly
  referencing get which mutates frequency)
- Remove redundant contains_key/get comparison code superseded by
  the peek-based approach
- Suppress unused result warning on peek() call in frequency test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
